user_input: What is the function of control loops in engineering diagrams?

Systems that maintain a process condition at a set value by adjusting devices based on feedback from instruments.

## References & Links

- [Instrument Schematics](./instrument-schematics.md)

- [Process Flow Diagrams (PFDs)](./process-flow-diagrams-pfds.md)', '# Logic Diagrams

- [Piping and Instrumentation Diagrams (P&IDs)](./piping-and-instrumentation-diagrams-p-ids.md)', '# SCADA Network Diagrams

response: According to the provided context, specifically in the document [equipment/control-loops.md](./control-loops.md), control loops are "Systems that maintain a process condition at a set value by adjusting devices based on feedback from instruments." This indicates that the primary function of control loops is to regulate and maintain a specific process condition by making adjustments based on the feedback received from instruments.
reference: Control loops maintain a process condition at a set value by adjusting devices based on feedback from instruments.
